g.data-narration: Linear Search is a fundamental searching algorithm that examines each element sequentially until finding the target.
g.text: Linear Search: A Fundamental Searching Algorithm

g.data-narration: Linear Search sequentially checks each element until target is found or list ends. Works on sorted and unsorted data.
g.text: Linear Search (Sequential Search) is the simplest searching algorithm that
sequentially checks each element until the target is found or list ends.
Works on both sorted and unsorted data | Time: O(n) | Space: O(1)

g.data-narration: Linear Search serves as baseline for comparing faster algorithms and works on unsorted data where Binary Search cannot apply.
g.text: • Serves as baseline for comparing faster algorithms like Binary Search
• Works on unsorted data where Binary Search cannot be applied
• Essential for linked lists and sequential access data structures

g.data-narration: Watch as the algorithm scans each element from left to right, checking for a match with the target value 86.
The algorithm starts at index zero, compares each element, returns index on match, or continues until the end.
The C implementation uses a simple for loop to iterate through the array, returning the index when found.

g.text: int linearSearch(int arr[], int n, int target) {

g.data-narration: Best case is O(1) when element is first. Average and worst case are O(n) requiring n and n/2 comparisons.

g.text: O(n) - Last element or not found

g.data-narration: Linear Search uses only constant extra space with just a few variables for loop counter and index.
g.text: Linear Search uses only a few variables (loop counter, index, target) - constant extra space.

g.data-narration: Linear Search is O(n) but requires no sorted data. Binary Search is O(log n) but needs sorted arrays.
Linear Search is simple, works on unsorted data, requires no extra memory, and handles any data type.
Linear Search is slow for large datasets, not optimal for sorted data, and requires sequential access.
Sentinel Linear Search places target at array end to eliminate bound checks per iteration, improving performance.
g.text: Places target at end of array to reduce comparisons - eliminates bound check per iteration
int sentinelLinearSearch(int arr[], int n, int target) {
int last = arr[n-1]; arr[n-1] = target; // Sentinel
while (arr[i] != target) i++; // No bounds check!

g.data-narration: Linear Search is the foundation for understanding all search algorithms with O(n) time and O(1) space complexity.
g.text: Linear Search - The foundation for understanding all search algorithms

svg.desc: Linear Search is a fundamental algorithm that sequentially checks each element until finding the target. Best case O(1) when element is at first position. Average and worst case O(n) with n comparisons needed. Space complexity is O(1) as it uses only a few variables. Works on both sorted and unsorted data.
